claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age category applies to your child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I'm looking to claim for my son Alex. He's 7 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you for that information! Since Alex is 7 years old, he falls into the **under 16** category, which means he automatically qualifies based on age.

Let me move to the next step of the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about Alex's work status:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Given that Alex is only 7 years old, I'd expect the answer is **yes** - he's not working. Can you confirm this?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - he doesn't work at all. He's just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's exactly what I expected! Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in his own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit claimed in his own name. Given Alex is 7 and in primary school, I'd expect he doesn't receive any such benefits. Can you confirm?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about apprenticeships:

**Is Alex in an apprenticeship?**

Given that Alex is 7 years old and in primary school, I'd assume he's **not in an apprenticeship**. Can you confirm this?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship - he's only 7!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! I apologise for the formality of these questions - I need to go through each step, but they're clearly not applicable to a 7-year-old! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we need to check your residency status. 

**Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ months)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources (and not claiming certain benefits)
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are a student with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, option 1 (normally living in the UK) is the most common.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, let me ask a follow-up question:

**Are you a Crown servant living abroad?**

Crown servants include people working overseas for the UK government, such as:
- Members of the Armed Forces
- Civil servants posted abroad
- Diplomatic staff

Or are you living in certain other qualifying countries under special arrangements?

If not, could you tell me a bit more about your situation? For example:
- Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?
- Are you working, studying, or seeking work?

This will help me determine if any of the residency paths apply to you.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I don't have settled or pre-settled status either. We just moved abroad for my partner's job.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, it appears you don't meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

Let me navigate to the outcome to confirm this.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry to share this news, but based on your circumstances:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result: Not Eligible

I'm sorry to let you know that **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| Alex | 7 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet UK residency requirements |

### Explanation

While Alex meets all the criteria for being a qualifying child (he's under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship), **you** as the claimant need to meet residency requirements to claim Child Benefit.

To be eligible, you must either:
- Normally live in the UK, **or**
- Meet special circumstances (such as being a Crown servant abroad, or having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme)

Since you've moved abroad for your partner's job and don't fall into any of these categories, you don't currently qualify.

### What You Can Do

- **If your circumstances change** (e.g., you return to live in the UK), you may become eligible and can apply at that time
- **Check the country you're living in** - some countries have social security agreements with the UK that may provide similar benefits
- **Contact HMRC directly** if you think there may be special circumstances that apply to your situation

I understand this isn't the outcome you were hoping for. Is there anything else I can help you with or clarify?

reasonings: Alex is 7 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship - so he meets all the child-related criteria. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad for their partner's job, is not a Crown servant, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. To claim Child Benefit, the claimant must normally live in the UK or meet one of the special circumstances (such as being a Crown servant abroad or having settled/pre-settled status with qualifying criteria).
arguments.overall_reasoning: The family has moved abroad for the partner's job. While Alex (age 7) meets all the child eligibility criteria, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements necessary to claim Child Benefit. The claimant does not normally live in the UK and does not qualify under any of the special circumstances such as Crown servant status or EU Settlement Scheme status.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad for their partner's job, is not a Crown servant, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. To claim Child Benefit, the claimant must normally live in the UK or meet one of the special circumstances (such as being a Crown servant abroad or having settled/pre-settled status with qualifying criteria).

While Alex (age 7) meets all the child eligibility criteria, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements necessary to claim Child Benefit. The claimant does not normally live in the UK and does not qualify under any of the special circumstances such as Crown servant status or EU Settlement Scheme status.
